seed_problem: Pack polyominoes into a W x H board maximizing covered area (score = 1e5*sum(k)/(W*H)), reflections allowed; checker validates no-overlap and in-bounds.
mutation: input: relax the empty board into an obstructed grid with sparse crevasses and a mixed catalogue of reusable polyomino footprints (rotations+reflections allowed) whose FIRST type tiles poorly; the goal stays maximum covered area, so a weak single-shape baseline is beaten by orientation-aware, value-density and gap-filling packings that diverge.
title: Glacier Sensor Net: Cluster Tiling for Maximum Coverage
strategies: baseline: stamp only footprint 0 (the plus pentomino) in its given orientation, row-major, wherever it fits
largest-first single-orientation greedy: at each empty solid cell place the largest catalogue footprint that fits using only base orientations
orientation-aware multi-pass packing: place footprints largest-first over all rotations/reflections, then run smaller-shape gap-filling passes to cover leftover pockets
randomized multi-restart repair: perturb placement order across seeded restarts and keep the covering with the most occupied cells
